text: It's not even a 1 star. This shxt ass phone company keeps robing me every month. Somehow my 130$ bill is $500 and not one of these dumb fxcks can explain where they got these numbers from. They hang up on you, barely speak english and plain fxcking suck. Go to boost

The customer service for T-Mobile is the worst I've ever had. I switched from Verizon which I had for many years but it became quite expensive. I wasn't unhappy with them at all. I was told with my magenta plan from T-Mobile that Netflix was included. When I was charged for the Netflix I was told I didn't have the magenta plan that includes Netflix. However, on their website they don't state this. After speaking to a manager she agreed to give me the "Netflix On Us" and needed to call me back to linkmy T-Mobile with my Netflix. She didn't call me back and when I called back several times the weight is an hour long. When I finally did get in touch with them Rachael the manager who promised the Netflix wasn't available and another manager told me that there was no way they could give that to me. I stated that I was promised from the other manager and they told me "well she gave you wrong information"  and they would not honor it. That manager put me on with his supervisor who was very rude telling me I can't always get what I want in life. I'm currently looking for another mobile service with good Custoner Service. When I mentioned this to the supervisor his response was "okay I can help you with that cancellation right now" Terrible!!

Calls drop, decided to switch carriers. Had their service for 2 days on a billing cycle and got charged the full month. I've called to try and dispute the charge, went to the Tmobile store. I've been told there is nothing they can do. Smh on them, I had them for over 10 years and couldn't even waive this rip off charge. Stay away from Tmobile.

In October I went to a T-Mobile store to pay my bill early and was charged extra. When I went back to the store the next day all the security settings on my phone was changed without my consent. I have spent 2 months trying to fix this problem and it appears to be impossible to fix. I don’t understand why this is happening

Do not, I repeat not go on with this company. You will want to shoot yourself in your foot. I bought the iPhone 11pro, in full. Got nothing like anything from any other iphone before. Run, run, run. This is a bait and switch at the very best. These ppl are gd thieves. Get out while u can. They are evil asses

Obtained a new line on Dec 5 with a new OnePlus 8t, one week later they release a new promotion that give you the phone for free with a new line. I called customer support to ask if the promotion can be upgraded for my account as i have been 5 years with them. They rejected my request saying that it cannot be done.

I cannot fathom how it cannot be done, clearly customer loyalty is not rewarded with T-Mobile. 

Coverage is also bad in my area, i like it for the price but I'm getting more and more disappointed with the service.
 
Consider other mobile providers if you want to avoid a bad customer experience.

Consistently some of the worst customer service I have ever had to deal with. Sometimes they even try to charge you for the pleasure, even though the free self service option on their website is broken...

Rafael the store manager at the T-Mobile 700 N. Michigan Avenue location is AMAZING!  After calling the T-Mobile 800 line 4 times & getting nowhere, I finally spoke with Rafael at the store.  I was a very irate customer, but he was polite & professional the whole time. (I did apologize) He did the best he could to rectify a messed up issue. Thank you, Rafael! Customer service is a rough job and he deserves a raise!  I hope his boss sees this and appreciate Rafael's hard work!
Bottom line is T-Mobile doesn't even deserve 1 star. Save yourself the aggravation and go with another carrier.

When I bought a prepaid sim card, they promised it would work nationwide, but it didn't work in Austin, TX. When I inquired about it, I got some lame excuse about improving the network. The agent was also rude, refused to help me and told me I should complain at the tmobile store where I bought it.

I have been a customer for 12 years. I called two months ago looking for a better plan. They told me they had a great plan for me with new phones. I told them I was an existing customer and they said my bill would $105/month so of course I said yes to this plan. However now my bill is  $230/month bc the plan they gave me is for new customers. Of course they say they can't change my bill and I owe over $300 which I can't afford. Going to cancel my service tomorrow since I spent two months trying to get them to make this right and they have done nothing except say sorry.  Should of went with Verizon like I wanted. Thanks for nothing T-Mobile.

Wow. I have previously had great experiences with Tmobile. That is to say, I have enjoyed their cheap prices and relatively solid coverage, however; this last month has been chaos. It had been a couple of years since I've used T-mobile. Although I had nothing against them, I moved somewhere that really only had good coverage by Verizon, so I switched to using them for 2 years. I bought a Tmobile phone from someone used on Marketplace and waited until they finished paying it off so that I would be able to request an unlock... Queue chaos.

First of all, the first time I called in, they said they would need the account owner to call it. I told them I didn't even remember the guy's name, and that it wasn't his phone anymore, so it doesn't matter. They reluctantly agreed and submitted the request. They said it could take 48-72 hours.

I got an email a week later stating that the unlock request failed because I didn't have an account with them for 40 days..... OF COURSE, I didn't have an account with them for 40 days. I don't have an account with them at all! The phone was paid off by him when the phone was on his account, now the phone is in My pocket because I bought it, and not on my account or anyone else. They said they would only be able to submit a higher-level request with their manager's approval, and I kind of sat there awkwardly for a minute before saying, "Well... can you get that then?" She said she would go ask. Came back a minute later..

Oh.. sorry to interrupt this riveting story, but, this was all after waiting on hold for an hour. I forgot to mention that. I waited on hold for an hour the first time, and an hour the second time to tell them that they messed up the first time so I was calling back to get it figured out. With that nonsense out of the way... Oh, wait.. One more thing. The reason I was waiting on hold for an hour each time was because when I talked to online chat - which I had to wait ~20 minutes to get through to someone and then we had a very cyclical conversation about if they were T-Mobile customer care or not because apparently I needed to contact T-Mobile customer care to request an unlock, and the people i were speaking with on live chat ON T-Mobile's website... were not T-Mobile customer care? Still unclear on how they thought that was obvious, but anyway. I called in, waited on hold, etc. etc... So she gets back, says the manager approved a "White list" and that it would take up to 7 days this time for the phone to be cleared on the white list and able to be unlocked. She asked for me to be patient, reiterated to wait up to 7 days, but that I would definitely get a text with a code and instructions for the unlock since this "White list" unlock had been approved.

I waited for 14 days. Today is day 14. I called back. I waited on hold - only 30 minutes this time, which I could honestly live with if it was always 30 minutes or less, but still... common.. You're T-Mobile, do you really not know how many calls you are going to get each day? Isn't that simple math? Average calls * seconds per call/seconds in rep's shift / reps... or something like that... Common guys.

Anyway.. I called back, 30-minute hold. They say they have to transfer me - I am thinking, please do not put me back in that 30-minute queue that I just waited in for you to say, "I'll have to transfer you" - Luckily they didn't and the person answered right away. I calmly and simply stated that I had purchased a used phone and I needed to request a carrier unlock -- since I have found in the past trying to explain and reexplain all of the previous interactions and ways they have messed up never seems to help a problem actually get taken care of faster and they think you are just angry about something that isn't their [personal] fault even though it is their [corporations] fault.

She simply states, "That person you purchased it from will have to be the one to request the unlock"... Me: Okaaay. Thanks. Them: "Okay. Bye"..... *click*

Now... I just want to be clear. I wouldn't actually be that upset if it were their policy that the person who paid off the phone has to be the one to request the unlock - although I think that is ridiculous because that guy doesn't even own my phone anymore, but he is allowed to make changes on it? And I'm not?!

But here's all I am trying to say... if that is their policy as a company -- as stupid as I think that policy would be if that really is their policy since they are denying the owner of the phone (me) the ability to request an unlock when I legitimately bought a phone that has now been completely paid off - which means they do not own it or have any right or claim to it anymore and I should definitely be able to unlock it and use it (my phone) with whatever carrier I want -- then that wouldn't be any of the 3 reps' I spoke with fault. But they didn't all say that. They all said 3 separate things. 3 calls. 3 days... 3 stories :/

Worst customer service on the planet, hidden fees, overpaying for terrible coverage that doesn’t work more often than not even in the city I reside in. Switch to Visible Mobile. $40 unlimited everything, a $100 gift card after second monthly payment plus a free fire stick (what other cellular service does that), better Verizon network coverage, keep your phone number (good luck getting your phone unlocked by T-Mobile) faster network and better customer service. Free SIM card and they overnight it for free. Super easy to switch. I told T-Mobile I’d be putting them on blast for putting me on a plan that cost more for options I didn’t need and was never advised of, three different people told me three different costs for the lower plan on same day, after they all said the lower plan doesn’t even exist, they lie. They have zero empathy for those struggling financially during this terrible pandemic. They could waive my monthly fee during the calif wildfires three years ago, but couldn’t help me out on a single months payment while 250k people are dying, most of us barely surviving on unemployment, and don’t have money to put food on their table. I’ve been a long time customer and they should have done the right thing, especially after ripping me off for services I didn’t know about. I’m now paying half of what I was paying and getting better coverage and free gifts from visible.com. Switch today and save! Breakup with T-Mobile for good!

First, I went to a t-mobile store where I stood (on crutches) for 10 minutes, no other people in the store, four employees, and no one helped me.  I left and called to sign up for t-mobile.  My plan was $40 cheaper than my verizon was.  However, it took 16 hours on the phone with customer service over two weeks to actually get everything working correctly and my bill right.  AWFUL support with technicians who don't seem to know how to fix problems.  Once I found a knowledgeable person, the fix took about two minutes.  But that was after going through many, many "experts" who couldn't figure it out.

Zero Stars....We first went to the new Glendale location... It was HORRIBLE! Employees were sitting on the floor. They were playing their own music very loud. The person who tried to help us page new iphone12 said he didn't know how to trade in our old iPhone so we would have to come back another time. It was so of the worst customer service we have ever had. 

We left and went to the Capital Rd location in Milwaukee. 5 STARTS

Kaitlynn is the BEST! We first went to the new Glendale location which was absolutely horrible!!!! We left and went to the capital location and thank goodness. Kaitlynn was incredibly helpful, knowledgeable and professional. If it wasn't for her we would have switched to a different company after now than 10years with T-Mobile..
I was truly impressed with the customer service I received with Renaldo at the North Miami Beach location. Thank you

I hope i can get my watch and iphone paid quickly because I am so over their horrible hold times.

Hold times for 60 to 90 minutes every time i call and this started even before the Christmas holiday with the awful hold times!

Then once i get someone ... they are just giving me a canned response and then get bounced around.

Issue not resolved.  Have a brand new iphone 12 and I rarely get over 1 mbps on the down stream and less than that for an upstream speed.  And before you say it.  Doesn't matter what time of day.  Can be 2 AM or 2 PM.

Funny two bars on this phone and should get at least 20 mbps on the download stream.

This was a really bad mistake to go to T-Mobile
T Mobile is terrible.  Switched from Verizon to T Mobile and my phone had no service more than had service.  Less than 2 weeks went back to Verizon and they billed for whole month.  Never go there!!

Tmobile is terrible. It's terrible in the store. It's terrible now that Sprint and Tmobile is merged. Everything is a pain to set up. Everything is a struggle. You have issues, good luck getting that resolved in a timely fashion. 

Here's our sad story. 
We switched from Verizon to Tmobile. We had Tmobile for a couple of years. We upgraded our boy's phones a couple of months prior to the merge. About a week before the two companies merged, a Sprint rep approached us with a deal. Never trust a deal that sounds too good to be true, because that usually is the case. The deal was that we send our phones to Sprint, who would then send us buy out money in the form of "gift cards" to pay off Tmobile for the phones. The boys would get new SE's and I would get an upgrade to an iPhone 11. Sounded simple enough. All was good initially. We got the phones, we set them up et al. 

It took about a month before we got the box from Sprint to send our phones back. We sent them back with the understanding that once they were received we would receive the gift cards to give to Tmobile and then pay them and close the account. We never got the cards. No follow thru was given to either of us "we received the phones, et al", nothing. 

In August our Visa was charged $800 by Tmobile because the payoff hadn't been made and we were on auto pay, so they were able to auto charge us. NO notice was sent about that either. My husband discovered this when he looked at our Visa bill. 

It's now December. Between he and I we have spent countless hours on the phone with Sprint/Tmobile trying to sort this out and be refunded our $800. The right thing for Sprint to do at this point is to credit our CC the $800. My husband is on the phone with them right now attempting for like the 5th time to get Tmobile to verify the charge to our credit card. That is finally verified, so now we have to wait and see what they're going to do about a pay off. They actually suggested issuing a credit to our Sprint account, which does nothing for us and our cc charge/interest, so we declined vehemently. 

This whole process was convoluted and over complicated. It continues to be so. The right hand in this "merged" company doesn't know what the left hand is doing. It is SO very annoying and frustrating. We have also started a dispute with our Visa card, so we are double fighting this. He's been on the phone for about an hour now just trying to get Tmobile to verify to Sprint that they charged us and how much. Ridiculous. 

Now they are telling the Sprint people that the charge was $798. Liars. Anyway, whatever, we'll eat the couple of dollars just to get the most money back. We already paid this, so just give us the money back as promised. I think we're still going to have to get Visa to twist their arm to credit our cc. This would be awesome, because they will charge Sprint/Tmobile for their time, which will make me feel better because we've been paying the interest on this for months now. Jerks. 

Look, if you're with another provider, just stay there. Sure, you may pay more, but the headache that you'll have to endure with Sprint/Tmobile (whatever, they are), is not worth the money you might save. Trust me on this one.

I am very disappointed with T-Mobile they don’t treat day old customers or value them like they should like this started in the beginning no good business very disappointing I will never recommend T-Mobile to anyone again very disappointed

Wednesday I was here 3:00 and half hours had to come back today and they still got me and other people waiting. 

Here I am again. Just don’t make no since. When are these companies going to ever learn. Pay your employees more money and stop trying to work the ones you have to death. SMH. 🙁
T-Mobiles customer service is the WORST I have ever seen!!!..I STILL HAVE NOT BEEN ABLE TO ACCESS MY ACCOUNT!...When I try to call them I get NOTHING BUT ATTITUDE FROM THEIR "EXPERTS!"...

They are liars . I made a mistake to switch from AT&T to T-Mobile. They promised me $250 per line to switch , and they never pay me. I spoke to customer service numerous times and every one is apologizing and promise you they will take care But it’s a bunch of lies. You can’t speak to the same people so you have to repeat the same story over and over and nothing gets done. The worse experience ever. Lie after another lie.

I was a previous T-Mobile customer. I left T-Mobile due to "lack of service", but I want to make it clear that T-Mobile has always had great customer service wile I was a customer. While I was a customer, I received awesome customer service. Once T-Mobile had reduced my bill from almost $200 to $75 because my phone was accidentally roaming. Moving to the reason why I'm writing this comment. Currently, I am not a T-Mobile customer, I've left T-Mobile for Verizon but I still have a range extender from T-Mobile. I am trying to send that item back to T-Mobile and they keep telling me that there is nothing they can do. They are telling me that I have to pay over $300 for this equipment that I have no use for. I feel like if I was still a customer or interested in returning they would have figured out a way to help return this item. Now that I am no longer a customer they have no requirement to me. I'm sorry T-Mobile but I think you may have lost my business forever.

They will put you into a lease to own agreement and by the end of the agreement they will tell you you need to pay almost full price for the phone. The service is very expensive and they some how found a way to buy out the best competition. My advice if your a customer buy your phone right now tell them to leave you alone and switch to AT&T at least they show their customers care and not threats during a pandemic and financial crisis. These guys are as bad as WellsFargo. Money Schemes are real.
Got the new s20  5g  and service and internet is worse then my s10 4g   really t mobile fix my plz

I spent 12 hours on and off in one day trying to simply pay for another phone because mine broke.  I got the run around all day being dished around from one specialist to another and then from one supervisor to another.  They couldn't figure out how to reset a code delivery using my email instead of my phone, which is broken.  I was stuck in this crazy loop....  

I asked them if they were hiring because I ended up getting to know a few of the reps.  Hey its me again!  Im glad I made a game out of it or else I would have lost my mind.  Maybe I was the lucky one.  I'll go and camp out at a store tomorrow.  This time Im taking lunch with me.  

New tag line:
Unbelievably terrible service.  All the time.

Sprint/T-Mobile service has been hideous.  After 2 months of calling their "Customer Service" dept MANY times (always winds being to a foreign country to a person who has no authority to rectify situation & who claims there is NO way for me to speak directly to a supervisor in the U.S.) - we are moving our family plan of almost 18 years to a new carrier.  Consumers- beware!  The saleswoman told me my phone would be "entirely paid off" in 24 months & made ABSOLUTELY  NO-NO-NO  mention of it being a lease.  Ultimately, my $700 phone has cost $1,300.  We transferred our contacts & photos to our  icloud accounts & we are dumping Sprint-TMobile.  Screw them.   We can take our $450/month & go with any number of other carriers.

Let me tell you about T-Mobile I don't BS people and I shot straight T- mobile has probably the nicest costumer service then any other phone carrier but they have to for the ride there fixing to take you on. They sell you service plans that don't even exist but in order for you to get that service plan you must sign up on auto pay that's because your service plan is fixing to change but if your already on auto pay your paying it my plan was 149.** I told them it couldn't be any higher that I was on a fixed income well the first bill was 189** and they started removing things from my plan to make it the 149** that's not what I agreed to one service member will do one thing and then another will do something else before you know it you don't really know what's going on all's I know is I had straight talk for 7yr paying for 2 lines and never had a problem I switch to T-Mobile and I'm getting bills for 400 but never did there costumer service really argue with me it's almost bewildering to you why it didn't work there so nice you want it to and there coverage area isn't what they say it is I got better service with my straight talk so I went back to straight talk and wished I had never been caught up in there niceness and promises they couldn't keep don't make the same mistakes

This company offers tremendous deals, great service, and great devices. But, as soon as you sign up they change the prices they told you, do not help whatsoever with the set up process, and add taxes and fees that are supposed to be included in the total AFTER you sign up then tell you “sorry someone didn’t tell you”. I have been with this company less than one week and it has been an absolute nightmare since the first day.

I have been a loyal customer of T-Mobile for years. I have recommended friends and family to this provider for years because of the great coverage for the price; however, I am extremely disappointed in T-Mobile's ability (or lack there of) to streamline solutions for its customers. Specifically, my situation is that I got divorced from my ex husband two years ago and have been trying to have my number reclaimed during that time. He remains the account holder, but removed himself from the account years ago. I have since maintained the account, paid the bills for the account, and have been the only person on the account for YEARS. I have continuously had issues getting my number transferred from the account because after COUNTLESS attempts on both T-Mobile's part and my own, my ex will not answer phone calls from either of us. Finally, after two years, an associate on my previous call (11/29) informed me that if, in 24 hours, my ex did not respond or release my number that I could submit my divorce decree as evidence to have my number transferred. Finally, I thought, there was hope. I called back today, 12/08, in hopes of finally resolving my issue. Ready to submit my divorce decree, I was informed that I needed to physically go into a store to present it just so they could call the customer service line (which I was already doing) to inform them of the documents. After I had already received the run around from both my ex and T-Mobile, this was extremely frustrating to hear. When I asked if there was alternative to physically going into a store, the alternative was to mail my divorce decree to someone in New Mexico. Shame on you, T-Mobile, not only is this 2020, but we are in the middle of a pandemic and you don't have a fax and/or email for your customers to send verification documents to instead of waiting five days for USPS or physically go into a store? After my experience with this issue, I will be looking at other options for a phone carrier and will be taking my family with me. I certainly hope that you all recognize that this is an issue and work toward a solution for ALL of your customers.

Sadly, I have been trying to close my deceased father's T-mobile account since two days after he passed away on October 5, 2020.  T-mobile has done nothing but cause me and my mother stress at this already difficult time in our lives.  I have forwarded the required documentation (death certificate, etc.) to T-mobile on three separate occasions, yet they refuse to close his account and turn off his phone!  Instead they insist on sending "Service Suspension" and Collection Warning letters to my Mother.  What kind of way is that to treat someone who is grieving for a loved one?  Out of all the things we have had to do to settle my father's affairs, this has been the most difficult and stressful; which makes absolutely no sense whatsoever, it is a cell phone, TURN IT OFF and STOP HARASSING MY MOTHER SHE IS GRIEVING THE DEATH OF HER HUSBAND!  I plan to call the corporate office tomorrow to file a complaint, I will follow up with a letter to the CEO, Mike Sievert.  Each time I called T-mobile (7 times), they offer me their condolences, but they don't care about me or my family or they wouldn't cause so much stress and just turn off the phone.

I obtained a new unlocked Razr 5G phone a couple weeks ago. It turns out, that TracFone/Verizon has SMS/MMS compatibility issues. So, I though I will give TMobile a try. The Magenta 55+ plan certain was enticing.  I did you TNobile, in the past, and I had to drop it, because their "system" would no longer support SMS for my work's alert system. I also dropped them, because I was part of the TMobile caused Experian breech.

Knowing what I was getting into, I called them to set up service. After a 45 minute wait, I was bale to get an account created. I was told a SIM would be sent to me via UPS overnight. The SIM never showed up. I could not check my order, nor could anyone in customer service.  All they could do is determined I had an account.

After getting no satisfaction, I ended up going to Google Fi. I could not get anywhere with TracFine either. I would not move my old phone number from TracFone to Google Fi Why? It woudl have been too painful to deal with TracFone off shore customer service. Probably teh same off shore customer service which TMobile uses.

So, I went with anew phone number and the $20 plan, wi-fi calling, and 1GB data. I do not use the phone that much, but it is needed at certain times.

So, if TMobile is the "best customer service", AT&T and Verizon must be even more horrible.

I initiated coverage and ported out from Verizon to T-mobile in August.  I could not get good service with BYOP program.  I tried a new T-mobile phone but did not like it.  Went back to Verizon after they cut me a good deal to return.  T-mobile billed me for 2 months even thought I had the phone for only 3 1/2 weeks. Their store agent assured me that I would only be billed for one month.  After 2 phone calls to T mobile account person,  they still persist in billing for 2 months.  Their service did not work, and they doubled billed me.  Very disatisfied.

Nothing with this carier is good 
The worst customer service experience!
Every 2 months "eccidentally" they add charges to my bill 
When I call to check why every representative giving me different answers until finally(after spending hours on the phone) the reason and they credit me in the next bill, on top of that I miss bunch of calls because they working on the towers for ever and it normal for them somehow.
And when I decided no more then start the most frustrating process I have ever deal with

Thought with the sprint merge it would be better, as in less dead zones for when my husband works cause he goes all over the state. HOW WRONG. Service won't even work in my own home. Takes 5min for texts to send. Send a photo? Gotta try at least 3x to resend before it goes through. Laggy gaming on the phone on the road or even home with wifi. Never had this many issues with a mobile phone company. Paying off phones asap and going back to AT&T or Verizon. Tmobile is a pos
Have tried calling their customer service at various times. Have waited over 90 minutes several times without getting through before giving up.  Going to change to a different provider.
How can t mobile has advertise good low cost plans? I understand having a cheaper service is good but only if you can get service. I find there service area very limited!!!

T mobile is a waste of money, been with them for 1.5 years and have had connection ploblems from day 1. I put up with a lot of took to long to load, and no reception. Now there texting don't work and there's no email for t mobile support . To call them they want you to have a different phone( if I had a different phone, I wouldn't have t mobile)so they can check the ploblems with your phone. I read there phone support is very bad too, I don't have time to waste being on hold. Time to go back to straight talk! I'VE NEVER HATED A SERVICE SO MUCH AS T-MOBILE !!

I have been a customer for over 6 years and I can honestly say that the customer service has gone to hell!!! My phone has been suspended due their error and will not work with me to resolve the issue. I have literally been waiting on hold for 2 hours!!!! Horrible!!!

Service used to be decent but after the Spring merger it has become pure garbage. always disconnects, cant hear caller, caller cant hear me. very unreliable. i am having to change over to another provider due to it being so bad.

Poor customer service. Extended hold times up to 1 hour off and on holds. Calls go to Philipines.  Promises made issues never resolved. Common sentences used by all customer services reps. I apologize, Not to worry, No worries, I will issue a credit for your problem,
Credit is never issued. No need to worry I will have a supervisor call you tomorrow.
It never happens. Just blony.
Phone call quality is not good. Customer service is bad. 
What happened to US Based customer support.
Got worse after Merger.
Old Business account customer.

This is fantastic. Seen some thing about Netflix. So tried tried tried and tried to contact Customer Service. Started with text
Keeping in mind i am logged in, TM asked me if I'm a member. Yes. You have to log in. So I log in
System said: got to renew  your password. Okay I love security. But, were do you think Cust. Ser. Went. A msg. left stating: be back, busy. 
   After waiting a half hour I called through the system. Told they would call back in an hour. Or TWO! Missed the first call...
 Went on voicemail at the same time they call again. Could not get out of the dam voicemail and lost call again. Called another Rep. Said: no problem! I'll cancel the first request you made and get you right over to technical support. 20mins. Later gave up
But not happy with T mobile. Why am I still doing this? Staying with frustration?
Can you really call this a service? Oh cmon. You've got a terrible customer service.

I wish I could give T-Mobile 0 stars honestly. I have never in my life had such a terrible experience with a phone company. I originally had sprint, until they stupidly merged with T-Mobile. Saturday, 11/29 my boyfriends iphone 11 completely broke. Front screen cracked to hell and back cameras cracked also. My boyfriend is a contractor and need his phone for inspections EVERY DAY. So I immediately went to T-Mobile where they told me they couldn’t help me due to us not being able to turn off his find my iPhone.. how do you do that when they are trying to send you a code to a phone that doesn’t turn on? Anyways, they directed me to Best Buy because they can apparently turn the find my iPhone off for me. I walked into bestbuy that same Saturday, for them to rudely tell me I have to make an appointment, which I did. I made it for the next day AN HOUR AWAY. I get to the appointment, just for them to tell me they don’t have the exact color of my phone.. why do you HAVE to get the same exact color? So, annoyed, I go to another bestbuy (another 30 minutes further from my house) where they tell me the same thing, they don’t have that color. Keep in mind each of these places can send me a phone via mail but that would take close to a week and a half.. AFTER we figured out the find my iPhone disruption. That Sunday, I made an appointment with Apple, which I couldn’t get in until Thursday. I had figured out how to turn off the find my iPhone on my laptop after 2 days of trying, just for Apple to tell me it didn’t work and I would have to do it again at the store :). Thursday rolls around and we make our way alllll the way up to the Apple store. They got us a new phone so quickly it was unreal! NO issues with the find my iPhone.. nothing! We paid the 100$ and got new accessories for the phone. Finally a sigh of relieve.. because it has just been a lot of back and forth at this point. Just when I thought it was all over.. Now comes the MOST annoying part. When we got the phone Apple told me I have to call sprint to get the sims card activated, which Apple said would be a breeze! WRONG. SO. VERY. WRONG. The first women I spoke to got the phone up and running within 5 minutes! We thought it was all over (again)! We used the phone for about 20 minutes before.. “invalid sims card” pops up on the screen again.z So I proceed to call again. This time, I was on the phone for an hour and a half. The gentleman that took my call proceeded to tell me that the sims card in the new phone DOESNT WORK. He said he can ship one out to me for 10$ and it would come in 3-4 business days.. I said no and asked to speak to a manager. The manager gets on the phone, SUPER nice guy! He assured me he would get me a sims card over nighted to my house for free and also credited my account 50$. Great! I was all for it let’s get this damn phone working already! He then transfers my call to a sales rep to get the sims card ordered (on the phone with them about an hour at this point). The sales rep proceeds to tell me THEY DONT HAVE ANY IN STOCK. That I wouldn’t be able to get one until the following week! What the actual hell! EVERYONE told me something different, literally EVERYONE I spoke to. Not only did he say that, but also “somehow” our call got disconnected. At this point I am crying out of pure anger. I tried to call back and again, disconnected before I could even speak to anyone after holding for another 20 minutes. I then used the chat, which they tried to make me pay for the shipping of the sims card that they now magically had in stock! They also said I wouldn’t get it until next week. I said forget about it I will go into the store myself AGAIN today. I will be getting the sims card (HOPEFULLY) and getting the hell off this plan. So much for Apple care and loyal customers! :)

Hello, I set up my account over 6 months ago, when I set it up I was told I have a tier 1 credit with a 4 or 6k EIP limit.  About a month ago I added a line for my daughter, and upgraded my phone, along with purchasing a tablet.  Everything went good.  About two weeks later I brought my wife into the store with me, which is difficult as we both work opposite shifts and I wanted to upgrade her phone.  We were told at the store that I did not have enough available credit, my limit was changed to $1800 but everyone I speak with told me that’s not right for all the lines that I have on my account.  I have tried to rectify this several times but each time I am told there is nothing that can be done.  I have 7 voice lines, two tablets and two apple watches.  Most of my devices are paid off, I have never missed payments and am wondering why my limit does not reflect the 4-6k as what was originally told to me, and from what all the reps are telling me my limit should be based on the number of lines.  They told me I can add a line and that should increase my limit, but I’ve added two additional lines for both my tablets and also added my daughters line and my limit did not increase? At this point I am looking at AT&T who already pulled my credit and is giving me a $5000 eip limit.  I don’t want to move 11 lines away from T-Mobile, so can someone please correct this for me? Every rep I speak with told me I have the best credit rating with T-Mobile and my EIP should be from 4-8k.  Please help me! All I want to do is upgrade one of my other phones.

Worst customer service, it is so hard to get tmobile representative on the phone let alone on chat,it takes about 1:45 minutes to get someone to take your call and even then they transfer you to a call center where they dont help you at all!! And you tru using the chat service it's even worse, they don't even answer. T-Mobile customer service is worthless.

NEVER, FOR ANY REASON, EVER USE T-MOBILE!!! I'VE BEEN A BUSINESS CUSTOMER FOR YEARS. LAST WEEK I ADDED 4 MORE PHONES TO MY ACCOUNT TO GIVE THEM MORE DATA - I HAD 10 GB PER LINE. WITHOUT MENTIONING ANYTHING, THE "BUSINESS CARE EXPERT" CHANGED MY GRANDFATHERED ACCOUNT TO A 2GP PLAN BECUASE I HAD MAXED OUT THE NUMBER OF PHONES ON MY BUSINESS PLAN. I HAD NO IDEA THAT T-MOBILE WOULD BE DUMB ENOUGH TO LIMIT THE NUMBER OF PHONES, BUT DO DOWNGRADE MY PLAN NOT ONLY WITHOUT TELLING ME, BUT SPECIFICALLY WITHOUT MY PERMISION IS COMPETELY UNACCEPTABLE!! THE FIRST "BUSINESS CARE EXPERT" I TALKED TO TOLD ME THERE WAS NOTHING SHE COULD DO BUT UPGRADE MY PLAN. WHEN I ASKED TO SPEAK TO HER SUPERVISOR, SHE CHIMED IN AS SHE WAS LISTENING AND SAID THE FIRST GIRL WAS RIGHT, I HAD TO JUST UPGRADE MY PLAN TO A MUCH MORE EXPENSIVE PLAN TO GET ABOVE THE 2 GB THEY DOWNGRADED ME TO!!! THE "SUPERVISOR WAS VERY ARGUMENTATIVE AND KEPT TALKING OVER ME AND EVEN CALLED ME A LIER SAYING "IF THIS ACTUALLY HAPPENED." I TOLD HER TO LISTEN TO THE CALL AS THEY ARE ALL RECORDED ANS SHE ASSURED ME SHE WOULD. THEN I ASKED FOR THE NUMBER FOR THE CORPORATE OFFICE, SHE TOOK A FEW MINUTES TO GIVE ME A DIFFERENT 800 NUMBER. I CALLED THAT NUMBER FROM MY LANDLINE AND SURE ENOUGH, IT WAS RIGHT BACK TO THE SAME CALL CENTER AUTOMATED PHONE SYSTEM TO GET BACK TO ANOTHER BASIC AGENT. I GOOGLED THE NUMBER AND GOT TO CORPORATE AND SPOKE TO KALEB WHO TOLD ME THAT "T-MOBILE CORPORATE POLICY IS WE DON'T PULL CALLS AT CUSTOMER REQUESTS" SO EITHER THE FIRST SUPERVISOR LIED TO ME OR CALEB DID. WE'LL SEE. BUT OF COURSE, T-MOBILE WOULDN'T WANT TO PULL CALLS FROM UNHAPPY CUSTOMERS THAT HAVE BEEN LIED TO OR MISINFORMED BECAUSE THAT WOULD MEAN THAT T-MOBLIE WOULD HAVE TO MAKE IT RIGHT, AND THAT'S OBVIOUSLY NOT THEIR BUSINESS MODEL. KALEB TOLD ME THAT I HAVE TO WAIT 2 DAYS FOR HIS SUPERVISOR TO CALL ME BACK, WHICH WE ALL KNOW WILL NEVER HAPPEN AND 7-10 BUSINESS DAYS FOR SOMEONE HIGHER UP TO DECIDE IF THEY ARE GOING TO PUT ME BACK ON MY OLD PLAN. YEAH, GOOD LUCK WITH THAT!!!!  NEVER, EVER USE T-MOBILE FOR ANY REASON - CHOOSE AN HONEST PHONE CARRIER!!!!
Horrible customer service. Management needs training on customer retention.

Suddenly an extra line has appeared on my bill. I didn’t ask for it and they claim that a fraud investigation is necessary. 
Why would anyone add a $20.00 line to a bill that is not attached to a device.
This mystery line materialized so as to coincide with a device upgrade. It takes hours to reach support if you reach them at all.
I've been a customer of T-Mobile for 10 years recently I have invested in the internet and t vision and this service is terrible I can't get any help And I've lost my internet for days at a time.

They made an agreement and are not honoring it. Do not agree for a trade in as they have and will not honor their offer.

Please read


Worst customer service ever

Costumer service is awful here they lie just to get anyone to switch to T-Mobile. They promised me that I would get the promotion they were offering if I switch that my other phones would get paid off since the amount I owed was less than $400 for each phone. Send me to a third party to unlock my phone which was a waste of $. They forgot to mention that if my phone was an LG they don’t reimburse for them. My phone is brand new it’s the latest model but that didn’t matter. Also the other phone in my plan was covered in their promotion yet T-Mobile keeps rejecting the promotion. Don’t fall for their promotions their nothing but lies. Wish I had never left AT&T. My bill is still really high which was the reason why I change phone companies and I regret it because there coverage is horrible I have no reception specially on base. I have also talked to other people and they had the same complaints 1 person even when back to AT&T and actually considering doing the same! If I could I would give them no stars at all.

I have only been with T-Mobile for a few months, and boy do I regret the switch! It has sucked all around for me. From them not honoring promos offered to randomly billing outside of my designated draft date. Not to mention poor phone service at times. I’ll be back AT&T!

I was suppose to get a call back at 6pm. The call back came at 5pm and I was unable to answer. Called customer service and was on hold for about 45 minutes when the call ended. I called right back and have been on hold now for 2 hours.

I just found out that anytime I talk to a actual person it cost me 20 dollars no matter on phone or in person at the store I didnt get T mobile I was a sprint customer and now looking at reviews Im scared

I have been trying to cancel my plan for over two months.  T Mobile would not send the pin to another phone on the account even though my 80 year old dad's phone literally broke into pieces.  They advised him to go into a store during a global pandemic--outrageous.  I have now been on hold for over an hour (after multiple phone calls).  I just want to cancel my plan.  How hard is it?  Are there that many other people canceling their plans that it takes this long?  The FCC needs to shut this company down.

I don't understand the 1.5hr. wait to s/w someone when TMobile uses more reps that are out of the country than a little bit!  Horrible CS.  Reps don't understand your inquiry.  Spends 10mins just getting the understanding of what you're calling for.

Was on hold with them for 2 hours just to get a representative to fix half my problem and the line disconnect and have to wait 2 hours and 45 minutes for absolutely no one to pick up. Worst automated system, if they are closed they should say so.

My experience with Anahita at the store in Northridge (0536) was awesome, because of her knowledge about the product and all deals. she was patient because she explained all difference between product of iPhone 12 with details. I got some idea after that I realized which one is the best option for me. Also, she helped me to place an order in the store which was prefect and I received my iPhone less than a week. I will recommend this store to anyone that is looking for great customer service!

I switched from Verizon because I thought T-Mobile is way cheaper. Well not really. When I came to the store they told me that for keeping my paid off device I am eligible for $400 credit card. They just need my last screenshot of the bill from Verizon. I did send them my bill and it has been a nightmare after that. Now they need to see all the bills from Verizon which i don't have access to anymore, customer service is sucks, don't even think that they will help you. They are unprofessional liars, and they told me i am not going to get paid for this month well guess what of course they charged me!

Don't get me wrong, T-mobile delivers their service, but their support is nothing short of terrible and mostly misleading. Their turnaround time to pick up the phone during work hours is generally great, but they do not have great technical support know-how and led me to unnecessarily reset my phone when I keep telling them the settings sent to my phone appear to be wrong and grayed out without giving me an opportunity to fix it. We're working on a BYOD that I know is unlocked. The same goes for their MVNOs. I've had nothing but trouble with bring your own phone. If you're using a phone issued by the carrier, you're probably going to be ok and have a better experience, but this whole BYOD is disappointing. Their reps claim they'll call back, but they never do. Then in their notes, the reps say they called back twice. It seems very dishonest to me how their support operations work.
Was just on hold with customer service for 2 hours and 45 minutes. Finally gave up. When I try to go online "they have trouble" pulling up my account.  Thanks for absolutely nothing tmobile.

T-Mobile pulled $812 dollars from my account when they should have pulled $153.  I was set up on auto pay.   I called in and was told a refund would be processed.   The refund never came, I've spent over 12 hours on the phone with T-Mobile over the course of 46 days.  I still haven't received the refund.  I disputed with my credit union, T-Mobile objected the dispute.   So in essence T-Mobile stole $659 from me (the difference between what they pulled out and my actual bill).   I've been trying to get a refund for 46 days.  I've been told 7 times, by 7 different supervisors, the refund has been processed.  And yet, 46 days later, I'm still trying to get the refund.   T-Mobile is the most dysfunctional business I've ever done business with.  They should be forced to close their doors.   They lie, they misrepresent, they have the WORST Customer Service I've ever experienced.  RUN, DON'T WALK, Get Away From T-Mobile as Fast As You CAN!
The coverage sucks and the prices are too expensive. Avoid them. They are basically a scam with how poor the service is and the cost.

Waited 45 min to speak with rep., I was charged 26 months for a service I never requested or received.  As I just noticed this, the offer was to credit 3 months, I asked to speak with a supervisor. Waited 10 min, disconnected. No one called me back. Called back in 10 min. Was told 75 min wait. Waited, answered my phone,  hung up on. I called back again.  75 min wait. However,  someone called back in 20 to 30 min. Explained what happened,  she Apologized and got me to supervisor.  Supervisor reviewed and saw transaction of adding service but no request from us to do so. Only took off 1 year credit for something we didn't request.  Should have offered at least 1 month credit for all I had to deal with. Also,  I've been with them since 1992, since before they were tmobile. They were Powertel. I requested new phone rates, nothing for being a long loyal customer,  just 500 off for Samsung. The supervisor was very nice but I just feel more should have been done. Looking at other carriers as I don't feel tmobile cares about a 30 year customer.  Will post this on customer review sites as well.

I got T mobile three days back. I received iPhone 12 Pro Max 256 GB ($1300) as per the plan. And immediately noticed scratch on the front screen. I called to T-Mobile customer service about that scratch and I was told T-Mobile will replace the device. I went to store as per the instructions from first call to verify the device, store representative checked the device and concluded as manufacturing defect and asked me to call T-Mobile about device replacement. 
I called to T-Mobile customer service again, and I was told phone will be replaced, but cost ($1300) will be charged on my account, I have to return the phone, after phone is returned the amount will be credited, and asked for the signing documents again. 
These guys sent me iPhone 12 Pro Max 256 GB with manufacturing defect, I spent three hours on the phone call with customer service, and one hour to store, still not able to fix the problem and asking for pay with return option and asking for the documents sign again. This whole process is crazy and decided to keep bad device. 
I felt I made bad decision to become a T-Mobile customer, and this whole thing is crazy.

I cannot strongly enough discourage any and all to avoid the new T-Mobile.

First, I was T-Mobile customer years ago and I was very much impressed by their customer service. I moved to a new location that had a worthless T-Mobile signal, so I had to switch to AT&T.

Years later, I rejoined T-Mobile. Despite their promises that they had a good signal, it turned out T-Mobile had a very inadequate signal at my new address. After 7 months of waiting for T-Mobile to provide the promised signal boaster, they told me to find another carrier. They were unwilling to compensate me for 7 months of crappy coverage.

When I left T-Mobile, my ex-wife spun off her own account, which was supposed to include her phone on her line on which she was making payments.

That same afternoon, I went online to pay off my balance. T-Mobile had left her phone on my account. I called immediately, and they agreed they'd made a mistake. After 30 days of them not being able to actually make this phone go to the ex's account because their computer system has/had a glitch, they offered to just give me a credit to zero out my account.

The next month, instead of a credit, T-Mobile doubled the charge. After many, many, many calls and countless hours of my wasted time, as well as a letter to the department which handles disputes, I was sent to collections.

Sadly, the company I had switched to was Sprint because their coverage map showed great coverage where I live (BTW: the area where I live has no access to broadband, so my phone is my only access to the world), but what their coverage map showed wasn't true.

A few months later (right after Sprint and TM merged), the closest tower to my house went offline due to a part that is indefinitely backordered with absolutely any idea by Sprint/T-Mobile when that tower will work again. No compensation for this either.

I went to Verizon. When I tried to pay off my Sprint leased phone and close my account, T-Mobile did everything within their power to keep my account open so they could continue charging me.

You cannot cancel your account in the app or online. You MUST call and wait and wait and wait to talk to someone who will tell you that you have to call another dept, and start the whole process over.

That would be bad enough, but even after you find the right dept and tell them to close your account, they refuse. You must provide irrefutable justification as to why, and they deemed my experience with them as not enough - so they just kept putting me on hold, transferring me around, and asking me over and over what they could do to keep me from leaving.

Did  NOT matter to them that my number was already ported to VZW weeks earlier. I spent hours on the phone, chat, and in the store before they would close my account and agree to stop charging me for service I was not getting or using.

This is the worst decision I have ever made,  their coverage is horrible,  5 g doesn't even work.  We've had little or no phone service since day 1.  We continued to call for help and they just said to be patient.   And we have been for 4 months.  Dropped calls, text messages not going through, calls going straight to voice mail.   Now they say we owe 4 thousand dollars.

Dear T-Mobile, on a recent visit to your Fort Lauderdale location on Federal Highway South, I had the pleasure of interact with Alyssa.  This professional was pleasant, polite, and super knowledgeable.
Her Alyssa's professionalism and knowledge made my visit easy and pain-free.  The member is a true asset and a credit to your organization.
Always respectfully,
Maurice Thompson

They will lie directly to you. Better have another person with you to help read between the lines like maybe a lawyer. One star is too good for the service you receive. They charge you on stuff they say is free. And for longer than the time expected. Over their business.

T mobile has the worse customer service i ever come across.
It took me 4 days to but the new Motorola razor 5g,because they won't allow the t mobile stores to sell them to customers. So you have to buy online . None of the customer service reps even no the phone exists,and they have no training on the phone. They refuse to send phones out to stores so customers can actually talk face to face in regards to the device. So I finally was able to buy a phone,and then I can't by accessories for it.
This is what happens when companies merge. They take a way the competition,and they get worse,and the customers suffer. Im going to switch carries ,because this is totally ridiculous after being a customer for 9 yrs

During the pandemic Tmobile is offering 2 payments instead of one to help with bills, however they kick you off the automated plan, you are unaware of this until your next bill is overdue which is a $10 late fee, its happened 2x to me although ive told them about it. Also, i switched credit cards and the keep attempting to use my old one. So the same thing is happening, and they are unaware unless i tell them. This is some Bank of America nonsense going on, where its a hassle to get the money back and they say it wont happen again.

DO NOT USE this company!! They won’t let you cancel and keep stealing your money. 
A lot of time wasted to try and get my money back that they were not Authorized to take out of my account. Horrible company.

Good evening to everyone that work for the T-Mobile  T-Mobile Customer Center. I do hope that all of yous will have a very  very  wonderful Tuesday ok smile. Also I do hope that all of yous are doing ok smile. In ending I want to tell all of yous that when my friend called the T-Mobile Support  Center Mr Richard the T-Mobile  Tech answered the Phone and my friend told him that she couldn't  get a complete phone call processed so Mr Richard took ownership of her phone problem and after a little trouble  shooting by Mr Richard  my friend was able to process a complete  phone call and I say thanks JOB WELL DONE ok smile. Last of all I want to say that Mr Richard is a very Positive Role Model for all young and old workers that work in the very very very   very busy T -Mobile Tech Support ok smile. Finally all of us say that Mr Richard is a  very very very very very Positive Representative for the T-Mobile Carrier Company that he works for day in and day out ok smile. Now last of all from all of us to all of yous please have a very  very wonderful Tuesday and also have a very Happy  Thanksgiving Celebration take care smile. 
Sincerely,
Elizabeth Hafford 
Angela Hansen 
Glen Hafford 
Morris W Conway Jr

text:
I have a Magenta Military plan.  I am supposed to be able to buy phones and add lines, but no luck so far.  I have been trying for a month with customer service, the phone app, and logged into my account on a laptop.  All I get is "we are unable to verify your information" message.  I am assuming they just don't want to live up to their original offers.  
Customer service says to go to a store and the activation fee of $20 will be waived.  I tried that, and they said no way we can waive the fee, and it will be $30 dollars.  This company really does not have their %#&&! together.  I will eventually leave when it becomes convenient for me.

okay so i just switched to t mobile after 3 years of having simple mobile which is basically the same thing but i do want to thank all the workers there for one raul was a huge help and is friendly as can be he assisted me and my boyfriend with getting our new devices and everything and had a lot a patients for us even tho we asked some pretty stupid questions Lol but i do want to thank him throught his app that i found we live in lebanon ohio and this is the best customer service i have ever ecperianced and hope it only gets better with every interaction with him thank you raul and i hope this review finds you soon you deserve it thank you and adam and Isaiah says hi and may many blessings come your way thank you and have an awesome day man -Isaiah lee maynard

Complete ass crack. The lady we were forwarded to was a bitchhh and wanted us to get the insurance for our phones but we said no and she kept insisting on it like 50 times. We were passed to the manager whatever and she was telling us the same thing so fudge you guys I hate t-mobile. Worst customer service. We used to be with sprint which was when everything would go smoothly for our phone trade in’s but now t-mobile ruined everything I hope you choke t-mobile eat shiz off my balls.

After months of working with tec support I'm still unable to log in. Also all my security info was sent to a wrong email address AND another phone number. I also recieved security info regarding someone else's account. Was promised on many occasions tec support would call me back and that never happened. I could not be more disappointed and disturbed about where my personal info went.

It is SO frustrating that you can never log in to see or pay your bill. I have to renew a password every time then try to log in right after renewing it and it says Oops somethings wrong.  Try to chat or call and its 45min to an hour wait.  Every single month I go through the same thing.  And trying to find the bill online now is like walking through a maze. My account is a business.  I would not recommend them anymore.  When you transact with them they send a survey right quick to see how your experience was and I used to put great.  Then it changed to good, then okay, now I'm just so over it.
I canceled my plan before the due date , they still charge my credit card . When I talk to customer service they lie and they are pathological lier .

We have been with T-Mobile since March 2019. We have had unreliable service and drop calls all the time even in our own home. I continually drop calls to my elderly mother. Even when visiting her, I drop calls from her home to my home. We must constantly re-start our phones, sometimes two or more times a day!

I cannot rely on T-Mobile when I need them the most. We call the company for help. Although not all, some of the reps are rude and disrespectful. I would not recommend T-Mobile to anyone.
Never ever use them. Overcharged us for years, after we had canceled they kept sending bills. Greedy duckers

Honestly I used to have no problems with Tmobile until they came up with their so called "promotion" of buy one galaxy watch get the second one free. The day that I decided to take advantage of the promotion the person helping me out charged me over six hundred dollars out of pocket for fees and taxes and I thought it ridiculous. I said no and I don't know what she did but she lowered it. Ended up calling me a day or two later telling me that she had made a mistake and asked if I could come in. I did her the favor and despite her mistake they made me pay for it upfront and in return they only discounted 50 dollars from one bill. And its funny since supposedly, the second watch was supposed to be free but I'm still paying for it. There was no discount, nothing. I'm paying for two full price watches for a supposed "promotion."
I went to store on Harrisburg pike bought a S20 from Moe and she was amazing.  Great experience. Ty again

If I could give a -star I would give it to this company. The worst service ever, I just switched from Boost Mobile, and I think this was the biggest mistake of my life. I have an hour on the phone waiting for a customer representative and nobody cares about me, the web page is down, so I am stock needing a service that this company cannot provide, this is incredible.... I will go back to Boost or Metro.

I just got off the phone with an customer service agent named Diamond. She was such a doll!!! I really appreciate how nice people are and this is why I’ve had t-mobile for 13+ years!!! Thank you!!! I really hope you see this Diamond

T-Mobile is suck. It has terrible signal and data speed is very slow. I can't get even 1mbps download lately and I am using galaxy note 20 ultra 5G. I only see a faster data speed after midnight. I live in Pomona CA 91766. Thinking to change to another carrier soon.

They claim to be the "best or biggest" everything, yet, you can't even get signal in a major city area. Their service is a joke.
If you need help from costumer service get ready, because their outsourced "experts" can barely understand you, and you may have some trouble understanding them. They are clueless.
I am so glad we have changed to a DECENT carrier and finally got done with T-mobile's terrible service. 
After cancelling out service, we kept getting charged for some reason, and costumer service again, couldn't do anything.
Never again.

I am a Sprint customer and have been since 2016. I recently sent my note 9 in to Asuncion to repair the screen. When I received the phone back they sent the wrong sim card back with my phone. Our local t-mobile store is closed for some odd reason so we traveled 40 miles to get my phone fixed. At first they were not sure but they put all their time and effort into restoring my service! Thank you Quad and Salvador! I appreciate you making something better in my day! Thank goodness 🙏

3 months ago I would have written a totally different review but the true colors have been shown. I have been a long time customer with t-mobile and it pretty much means nothing. I've been told that there is nothing they can do to resolve my issue and it is what it is. I have never been late on my account and now not only will I be late they are stating the account will be sent to collections. After the money is collected they will not help you, whether you are in the right or wrong you will lose. I will be leaving t-mobile and will not recommend this company to anyone again.
Stay away from T mobile all they can do is lie to you they told me I had 30 days to back out I called the same day to back out now they want me pay for a service I never use

The website is a nightmare. I log on, only to tell me that page doesn't exist (whatever supposed to come after typing in the password) . I click "my account" and while it looks like I logged on, I don't see any information about my account until I click back and forth and maybe even do a summersault, followed by  round house kick. Also, the treatment of prepaid customers (aren't we all prepaid?) seem to get a treatment of the plague or something lesser on their cast system. 
I couldn't even upgrade to a new device or plan, the site seem to crash or won't allow me to navigate around and it's very slow.  Whoever coded this should be fired , like immediately. I would. 
Their app doesn't even work at all. It just advises to go visit tmobile dot com to check my acct. This level of incompetence at during when everyone is home and most stores are closed do to the virus-  has to be a business killer, I can see people turning back and away from this site like 9 out of 10, and the one remaining is a software engineer or a hacker.

It covers the entire time I've had the service I signed up and was supposedly given an exact monthly cost then from the very first bill its been no less than 50 more than that the bill monthly is half my mortgage cost then they give me none of the current promotions because I'm not a new customer so you over charge me month after month constantly sending reminders days before the bill is due as of I need to be reminded that your ripping me off i wouldn't recommend this company to my worst enemy I absolutely regret leaving my old company to join a company that has cell service that only works when your home this company is trash

Awful. Was told an item would arrive two days later. Four days later, no item, and the tracking # issued by T-Mobile drew a blank on their own computer. After three and a half hours on the phone with multiple agents, no remedy. I went to a store, and the replacement item they found was "beyond its expiration date" and therefore not able to activate. To boot, the agent in store had his mask hanging around his neck at the peak of Covid-19. T-Mobile is like a sick octopus. Multiple legs and totally uncoordinated.
lock me out of my own account for 24 hours with no options to login other than call and be on hold for 30 years.
We brought a Chatham wells mattress and box spring paid over 3000 dollars we have it 6 months it' sags on both sides dont recommend it

I had sprint and it's the worst crap u can get so when t-mobile took over I was hoping for the best but nope my service is even worse now and they won't do anything about it besides tell me to reset my phone

edits.text: I had stupid sprint and now that it's t-mobile seems like it's getting worse and all they tell me is too reset my phones and if I live by any mts. or hills now  I'm stuck with horrible phones that don't work half of the time DO NOT FALL FOR THEIR DEALS YOU WILL REGRET IT

text: I have had tmobile 2 and half years and since the pandemic all they have done is screw thing's up and tell lies. THEY FIND WAYS TO MAKE YOUR BULL HIGHER AMD RIP YOU OFF! GOOD BYE TMOBILE YOU ARE A HORRIBLE COMPANY!

We actually really liked our service with T-Mobile until Monday 11/10. Apparently they decided that we sent spam and cut off my ability to send text, 2 days before we were going on vacation. We have a special needs grandson and we also have an 8 month old grandson. Apparently, I am a spammer. Really? Who has time for that. At any rate, I just got one of those watches so I am on the hook for a year or two....for the watch. Good luck with that $13.00. Looking at new phone service.
